item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoy this one. I hope you're happy with it, because you certainly have the right to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this up until the exploration log, then it becomes a cliche MTF slaughter with the worms acting like generic evil monsters. That final addendum switched my upvote to a downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyable. The only part I disliked was when it said the video feed cut off after the subject was attacked by the entities, it felt cliche. Other than that, good job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the critique. It would be hard for me to "fix" it though, as the ends of Tapeworm and Hookworm were used as plot-devices to reveal more about the skip (I mean, Tapeworm didn't technically die, but I still see where you're coming from).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be very good if the expo. log wasn't so cliche. It is exactly the same as most of the others, and that is bad. The worst part is that it falls for the troupe that the MTF always dies. -1 for now, but if you fix the log, it would be amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the critique. Since that was indeed a common complaint I've been getting, I decided to trim down the test logs into a different format (now detailing "Noteworthy Structures" instead of the whole test). As for the interview log, I did trim it down since it was another common point, but it wasn't a huge trim. I don't know what I could trim while making feel complete, and I did consider getting rid of it all-together, but that would probably be an excessive decision on my part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea, but I feel like the addenda overstayed their welcome and generally overlapped. They didn't exactly repeat themselves, but it felt like they retreaded a lot of the same conceptual territory (for instance, the interview and testing logs are both seem to be depicting the same general sequence of events multiple times with only a few differences.) I think this piece would be a lot stronger if you condensed the addenda, maybe into a few shorter tests (with a focus on an interesting facet instead of trying to capture the entire test) and then the exploration log? I think that's how I would go about it at least.

I'm at -1 until that whole section is tightened up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the critique. Dialogue definitely isn't one of my stronger points. The intent of the Interview was just supposed to be a little bit of buildup to the worms having a greater control over what goes on in the infected locations (the mummy puppet, specifically). It does kind of drag on, and I might revise it to be an easier read. I might remove it full-stop and make a different one that gets that buildup across better, but that could take some time.

Embarrassingly for me, I never read 3000. I might revise the exploration log here too, just so it isn't too similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I think this skip has an undeniably strong start, but that dithers as you hit the Interview Log. The dialogue was just not up-to-scratch as far as realism goes- not to mention what a drag it was to read. The whole time I just kept thinking to myself, 'Is this really adding anything?' I felt as though the description did sufficiently well at explaining the concept without needing this elaboration that the interview provided.

Barring that, the ending was wonderfully ominous, and I certainly didn't see it coming. It was a little reminiscent of SCP-3000's first addendum, but ultimately not enough to detract from my reading experience. Good work.

item.title: First Skip
item.content:encoded: Thank you to all who reviewed the draft, namely Leveritas, who suggested the idea of the Foundation looking into its origins.
